content: Options exit-management norms (tastytrade/premium-seller canon, cross-checked): dual rule 'close at 50% profit OR 21 DTE, whichever first' — the 21-DTE rule avoids terminal gamma acceleration, 50%-target maximizes P&L-per-day-in-trade vs holding to expiry. Defined-risk spreads support 25-50% targets (width/commission dependent). These are ENTRY-side canon but the exit logic (profit ladder + time/gamma stop) is exactly what an exit advisor encodes; the user's playbook (+5/+10/+15% takes, -25/-50% stops, EVT<=3d, GAMMA front<7DTE) is a personalized variant of the same skeleton.
